# mos_eisley — Nano CITY paint brief (GENERATED by gen_city_paint_brief.py)

DO NOT HAND-EDIT — re-run the generator to refresh. Placement, district
character, and per-landmark visuals are all derived from the city map
(maps/mos_eisley.yaml), so this prompt cannot drift from the map.

- **Feed seed:** `static/tools/seeds/mos_eisley_tight_seed.png` (the city tight seed)
- **Aspect:** 1.046:1
- **Save painting to:** `static/maps/mos_eisley_substrate.png`
- 7 key landmarks, 53 background rooms, 7 districts.

## Prompt (paste verbatim into Nano/Gemini with the seed attached)

```
Concept art / environment plate for the map screen of a science-fantasy
role-playing game. BASE LAYER ONLY. The image must contain absolutely NO
TEXT of any kind — no labels, letters, numbers, signs, legend, or compass
markings. Names are composited later; a clean text-free plate is the point.

Use the attached base image as the EXACT, FIXED spatial layout. Preserve the
composition precisely — do NOT reflow, rescale, rearrange, or re-proportion
anything. Specifically:
  - the bright lines are the main roads/routes: keep them running exactly
    where they are;
  - each solid colored region is a zone: keep its position, size and shape,
    repainting it as ground/terrain of the character described;
  - the large GOLD blocks mark key features that MUST be clearly visible:
    paint a distinct feature of the type described on that exact spot;
  - the faint pale rectangles are background density: render them as ordinary
    structures filling that area — do NOT make each one a distinct landmark;
  - any marker at the very edge is an off-map direction, NOT a building.

Style: hand-painted tabletop-RPG sourcebook cartography — painterly,
weathered, warm and tactile, like a printed campaign atlas. Top-down with a
gentle ~10-15 degree oblique tilt so structures show a little height.
Cohesive limited palette. Render at 1.046:1 aspect ratio, filling the frame
edge to edge.

Setting: a lived-in, used-future space-opera world. Low-tech, weathered,
functional. No modern Earth elements, no automobiles, no contemporary
signage, no soldiers or uniformed troops, no franchise iconography.

Subject: MOS EISLEY — a lived-in frontier settlement. The town is built up across the whole map; keep the roads and open ground between districts clear.

Districts — repaint each colored region in place as its ground type: SPACEPORT (lower-center): an open landing field of circular docking pits and parked freighters; MARKET QUARTER (center): a dense crowded market quarter of stalls and low blocky buildings; CANTINA ROW (left of center): a seedy strip of cantinas and dives; CIVIC (upper-center): a small orderly civic quarter; OUTSKIRTS (center): thinning ramshackle outskirts fading toward open ground; JUNDLAND WASTES (right of center): rocky badlands and broken canyon country; DUNE SEA (right of center): open empty sand dunes.

Placement — paint each named feature ON its gold block, nowhere else: DOCKING BAY 94 lower-left; LUCKY DESPOT lower-center; DOWAGER QUEEN center; CHALMUN'S CANTINA left of center; KRAYT GRAVEYARD far-east edge, mid-height; JABBA'S TOWNHOUSE lower-center; HYPERSPACE BEACON upper-left. Access markers: JABBA'S PALACE toward the upper-right (an off-map direction — paint only a road/trail leaving the frame that way, not a structure); THE SARLACC toward the far-east edge, mid-height (an off-map direction — paint only a road/trail leaving the frame that way, not a structure).

Paint these specific key structures (and no other major landmarks — the faint pale rectangles are ~53 ordinary background buildings, fill them as generic structures, NOT distinct landmarks):
  • DOCKING BAY 94: a circular landing pad / docking berth for a starship.
  • LUCKY DESPOT: a large grounded vessel repurposed as a structure.
  • DOWAGER QUEEN: a half-buried old starship wreck jutting from the ground.
  • CHALMUN'S CANTINA: a notorious cantina, the busiest dive around.
  • KRAYT GRAVEYARD: a field of giant bleached skeletal bones in the open desert.
  • JABBA'S TOWNHOUSE: an opulent fortified townhouse of a local crime boss.
  • HYPERSPACE BEACON: a tall slender navigation/comms tower.

Final reminders: NO TEXT anywhere — no labels, legend, compass letters, grid,
numbers, title, signature, watermark, or border. Clean painterly terrain to
every edge.
```
